session_1.speaker: Alice
session_1.text: I just got back from my trip to Kyoto last week. The bamboo forest at Arashiyama was incredible.

session_1.speaker: Bob
session_1.text: That sounds amazing! How long were you there?

session_1.speaker: Alice
session_1.text: Five days. I stayed at a ryokan near Fushimi Inari. My room was called the Sakura Suite.

session_1.speaker: Bob
session_1.text: Did you try the local food?

session_1.speaker: Alice
session_1.text: Yes! I had the best ramen at a tiny place called Ichiran on my second day. Cost about 1200 yen.

session_1.speaker: Bob
session_1.text: What about work? Are you still at the same company?

session_1.speaker: Alice
session_1.text: Yes, I've been at Tanaka Corp for three years now. I was promoted to senior engineer in January.

session_2.speaker: Bob
session_2.text: Hey, what stack do you work with at Tanaka Corp?

session_2.speaker: Alice
session_2.text: Mostly Python and Rust. We switched from Go last year because of memory safety requirements.

session_2.speaker: Bob
session_2.text: What's your favourite thing about the new stack?

session_2.speaker: Alice
session_2.text: The Rust borrow checker forces you to think about ownership which actually catches bugs early.

session_2.speaker: Bob
session_2.text: Didn't you mention you have a cat?

session_2.speaker: Alice
session_2.text: Yes, her name is Mochi. She's a three-year-old orange tabby. She knocked over my laptop last Tuesday.

session_2.speaker: Bob
session_2.text: What do you feed her?

session_2.speaker: Alice
session_2.text: She only eats Royal Canin Sensitive. Anything else upsets her stomach.

qa.question: Where did Alice stay in Kyoto?
What is the name of Alice's cat?
How long has Alice worked at Tanaka Corp?
What programming language did Alice's team switch from?
How much did Alice's ramen cost?
What cat food does Mochi eat?
When was Alice promoted to senior engineer?
What did Alice think of the bamboo forest?
Would Alice recommend Rust over Go for safety-critical software?
What did Alice's cat knock over?

session_1.speaker: Carlos
session_1.text: I finally finished building my home server last weekend. Took me three months.

session_1.speaker: Dana
session_1.text: Nice! What specs did you go with?

session_1.speaker: Carlos
session_1.text: AMD Ryzen 9 7950X, 128GB ECC RAM, and four 8TB drives in ZFS RAIDZ2.

session_1.speaker: Dana
session_1.text: That's serious hardware. What are you running on it?

session_1.speaker: Carlos
session_1.text: Proxmox for virtualisation, with a Plex media server, Home Assistant, and a self-hosted Nextcloud.

session_1.speaker: Dana
session_1.text: Do you have off-site backup?

session_1.speaker: Carlos
session_1.text: Yes, I sync encrypted snapshots to Backblaze B2 every night at 2am.

session_2.speaker: Dana
session_2.text: What's the power draw on that server?

session_2.speaker: Carlos
session_2.text: About 180 watts idle, 350 at peak. My electricity bill went up about 40 pounds a month.

session_2.speaker: Dana
session_2.text: Worth it for the privacy though. Are you running any AI workloads?

session_2.speaker: Carlos
session_2.text: Yes, I have Ollama running Llama 3.1 70B quantised. My wife uses it for recipe generation.

session_2.speaker: Dana
session_2.text: What GPU?

session_2.speaker: Carlos
session_2.text: Two RTX 4090s in the same machine. Overkill maybe but I wanted to run 70B without offloading.

session_1.speaker: Eve
session_1.text: I defended my PhD thesis last Friday! Six years of work finally done.

session_1.speaker: Frank
session_1.text: Congratulations! What was your thesis on?

session_1.speaker: Eve
session_1.text: Adversarial robustness in vision transformers. Specifically defending against patch attacks.

session_1.speaker: Frank
session_1.text: Who was your supervisor?

session_1.speaker: Eve
session_1.text: Professor Chen at MIT. She's been working on adversarial ML since 2015.

session_1.speaker: Frank
session_1.text: Are you staying in academia or going industry?

session_1.speaker: Eve
session_1.text: I'm joining Anthropic as a research scientist in September. Safety team.

session_2.speaker: Frank
session_2.text: That's a great fit given your adversarial robustness background.

session_2.speaker: Eve
session_2.text: Exactly what they said. The offer was 310k base plus RSUs.

session_2.speaker: Frank
session_2.text: Are you relocating?

session_2.speaker: Eve
session_2.text: Yes, moving from Boston to San Francisco in August. Already found a flat in the Mission district.

session_2.speaker: Frank
session_2.text: Are you bringing your dog?

session_2.speaker: Eve
session_2.text: Yes, Max is coming. He's a two-year-old border collie. The building is pet friendly.
